page: Microsoft Azure E4-2as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Easv7 server family. It features an x86_64 AMD processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The server is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 16.0 GB of RAM per vCPU core.

This instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options, though the hardware is Premium Storage capable. There are no onboard GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration. The lack of local disk storage and hardware accelerators represents a clear tradeoff, positioning this server away from high-speed local caching or parallel computing tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio allows workloads with large memory footprints to run on fewer CPU cores, potentially reducing software licensing costs that scale per core. This resource profile is suitable for memory-intensive workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caching layers, and enterprise applications that require substantial memory capacity without demanding high parallel processing power.
description: Microsoft Azure E4-2as_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the Easv7 family, powered by an AMD processor and running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a high resource density of 16.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying instead on remote Premium Storage capabilities for data persistence. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that are constrained by memory capacity rather than compute power, allowing organizations to optimize per-core software licensing costs. The server is configured for memory-intensive applications, including relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise workloads requiring substantial memory allocations.
